page: Microsoft Azure D8pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 server family. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. The system is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard compute workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is 2073.5, and the multi-core score is 16568.19. Single-threaded gzip compression stands out as a strong point, achieving 33.13 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% of tested hardware. However, cryptographic operations represent a relative bottleneck, with the PassMark CPU encryption test yielding a weak score of 1511.24. Memory performance is stable, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 909.92 GB/sec and uncached large-block write bandwidth at 72.69 GB/sec.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D8pls_v6 is a cost-efficient option for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. It is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 15.32 GB/sec, and lightweight database operations, processing 16.49 million Redis SET operations per second. It can handle small-scale language model inference, though performance drops significantly on larger models, as shown by a poor prompt processing speed of 12.34 tokens/sec on a 7B model.
description: The Microsoft Azure D8pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dplsv6 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:2 memory-to-core ratio, and contains no local storage or GPUs. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server delivers average multi-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 16568.19 and a strong single-threaded gzip compression speed of 33.13 GB/sec. Conversely, cryptographic performance is a notable tradeoff, scoring weakly in encryption benchmarks. With its dedicated ARM architecture and low-memory profile, the D8pls_v6 offers a cost-efficient solution for compute-focused workloads with modest memory requirements, such as static web serving, Redis database operations, and lightweight containerized applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Neoverse-N2 vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for compute-focused workloads under Hyper-V.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8pls_v6 is an ARM-based VM with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 3.4 GHz Neoverse-N2 processor, optimized for compute-focused tasks.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores and a low-memory profile optimized for compute-focused, scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor
16.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 33.13 GB/sec
No bundled local storage or integrated graphics processing units
Suited for static web serving, Redis operations, and lightweight containerized tasks
